name: Full Name
definition: Official protein name of the cargo protein containing the NES.
rationale: Identifies the protein for which nuclear export signal (NES) is annotated, enabling linking to other protein databases and literature.

name: Alternative Names
definition: Other names or aliases for the same cargo protein (e.g. synonyms, historical names, isoforms).
rationale: Provides flexibility for matching literature or databases that may refer to the same protein by different names.

name: Organism
definition: Source organism in which the NES-containing cargo protein was experimentally characterized.
rationale: Important for interpreting export data and assessing conservation or species-specificity of NESs.

name: Experimental Evidence for CRM1-mediated Export
definition: Type(s) of experimental evidence supporting CRM1-dependent nuclear export of the cargo (e.g. leptomycin B sensitivity, localization assays, mutagenesis, binding assays).
rationale: Distinguishes validated NES cargoes from putative ones, enabling confidence assessments and filtering by experimental support.
allowed_values: Reporter activity when co-expressed with CRM1
Mislocalization in Xpo1-1 temperature sensitive strains
Inhibit or inhibited by other CRM1 cargos

name: Mutations That Affect Nuclear Export
definition: Reported mutations (amino-acid changes) in the cargo protein that impair or abolish its nuclear export (i.e. NES functional mutations).
rationale: Identifies critical residues for export, allowing mapping of functionally important positions and validation of the NES region.

name: Mutations That Affect CRM1 Binding
definition: Reported mutations in the NES (or cargo) that specifically impair binding to export receptor CRM1 (without necessarily abolishing export).
rationale: Provides insight into the molecular determinants of NES–CRM1 interaction, useful for structure–function analyses and modeling.

name: Functional Export Signals
definition: Sequence positions (start/end indices) of the experimentally validated Nuclear Export Signal within the cargo protein.
rationale: Pinpoints the exact region responsible for export, necessary for sequence-level analyses, motif discovery, or computational prediction.

name: Secondary Structure of Export Signal
definition: Structural context (e.g. predicted or known secondary structure) of the NES region (e.g. helix, strand, coil).
rationale: Provides information about conformational context of NES, which affects its accessibility and binding to CRM1; helpful for structural and biophysical analyses.

name: Other Residues Important for Export
definition: Additional residues (beyond the core NES) reported to influence export — e.g. flanking residues, non-hydrophobic residues, or context-dependent residues.
rationale: Captures more subtle contributions to export functionality (e.g. residues that modulate NES strength or regulation), enabling more nuanced understanding and better predictive modeling.

name: Sequence
definition: Full amino-acid sequence of the cargo protein.
rationale: Provides the context for the NES, enabling domain annotation, mapping of NES region, evolutionary comparison, and structural predictions.

name: is_NesDB_doubt
definition: A flag or indicator of quality/confidence (or doubt) regarding the validity of the NES annotation in this entry.
rationale: Allows users to filter out entries with uncertain or disputed evidence, improving reliability when constructing benchmark sets or training predictive models.
